source_jobs.title: People Manager (Generalist)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PAVE BANK:

Pave Bank is building the financial infrastructure for a new generation of global users — where traditional banking, digital assets, and modern payment systems converge.

At the same time, we’re building a People function designed to match that ambition. We see People not as a support function, but as a core driver of how the company scales — from hiring and organisational design to culture and performance.

We’re assembling a high-calibre team of operators and builders who are excited to shape both the business and the environment they work in, with the opportunity to build systems, processes, and culture from the ground up.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

Talent Acquisition

 - Own end-to-end recruitment across all functions (sourcing to offer)

 - Partner closely with hiring managers and leadership on hiring strategy and workforce planning

 - Build and maintain strong talent pipelines

 - Ensure a high-quality, consistent candidate experience

People Operations & Payroll

 - Oversee day-to-day People operations across the full employee lifecycle

 - Manage payroll processes, ensuring accuracy and timeliness

 - Maintain employee records, contracts, and People systems

Compliance & Policies

 - Ensure compliance with local labour laws and regulations

 - Develop, implement, and continuously refine People policies and processes from scratch

 - Serve as the internal point of reference for governance and best practices

Employee Relations

 - Act as a trusted point of contact for employee matters

 - Handle employee relations issues with professionalism and discretion

 - Drive engagement initiatives and support a strong, healthy culture

Strategic Business Partnering

 - Work closely with leadership as a strategic People partner

 - Support organisational design, performance management, and scaling initiatives

 - Provide insights and recommendations on people-related decisions

Executive / Operational Support

 - Provide ad hoc support to leadership (e.g. scheduling, coordination, follow-ups)

 - Assist in internal communications and cross-functional initiatives where needed




REQUIREMENTS

 - 5–8 years of experience in People / HR, with strong exposure to recruitment, operations, and generalist responsibilities

 - Proven experience building People functions, processes, and policies from 0 to 1 in a startup or high-growth scale-up environment

 - Strong experience working in globally distributed teams and hiring across multiple geographies

 - Demonstrated fluency in global hiring practices and international talent markets

 - Strong understanding of Singapore and Malaysia Employment Act; exposure to additional international employment frameworks is a plus

 - Hands-on experience managing payroll and ensuring multi-country compliance

 - Strong communication and stakeholder management skills across diverse, international teams

 - Highly organised, proactive, and comfortable operating in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment

 - Ability to balance strategic thinking with hands-on execution




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience managing regional or multi-country People operations

 - Prior experience in fast-scaling startups or venture-backed scale-ups

 - Familiarity with modern People systems and tools such as Ashby and Revolut People

 - Prior experience working closely with founders or senior leadership in a high-growth environment

 - Strong exposure to building or scaling hiring engines for global teams (EMEA, APAC, US coverage)

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PAVE BANK

Pave Bank is the world’s first programmable business bank. Built by the founders behind Monzo and BigPay, we’re redefining global business banking by combining programmable financial infrastructure with world-class client experiences.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Pave Bank is building the next generation of programmable banking infrastructure. We are looking for a Senior Software Engineer to join our Engineering team and work closely with Product, Compliance, Treasury, and Operations.

In this role, you will play a pivotal role in shaping the backbone of Pave Bank, delivering scalable, reliable, and maintainable systems that power our core banking operations. You will design distributed systems, implement features end-to-end, mentor junior engineers, and collaborate across teams to drive high-quality software that accelerates the business.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design, build, and maintain backend services using Go or other strongly typed languages.

 - Develop scalable, event-driven distributed systems that power critical banking operations.

 - Collaborate with stakeholders (Product, Compliance, Treasury, Operations) to define requirements and deliver high-quality solutions.

 - Write detailed design documents for new features and system improvements.

 - Review pull requests and provide constructive technical feedback to peers.

 - Implement end-to-end features: architecture → coding → testing → deployment.

 - Ensure code quality through clean, maintainable, well-documented code and automated tests.

 - Contribute to engineering best practices, tooling, and system reliability improvements.




REQUIREMENTS

Technical Skills

 - Minimum 5 years of professional software engineering experience.

 - Strong proficiency in Go, or proven exceptional ability in another strongly typed language.

 - Solid understanding of distributed systems and event-driven architectures.

 - Experience in a Tier 1 tech company (e.g., Stripe, Grab, Google, Deliveroo, Monzo, Shopee, Bytedance) or significant open-source contributions.

 - Hands-on experience with Temporal, GCP, or Encore.dev http://Encore.dev is a plus.



Non-Technical Skills

 - Collaborative, empathetic, and able to communicate effectively with technical and non-technical stakeholders.

 - Strong interest in fintech, banking, or financial infrastructure.

 - Comfortable navigating ambiguity and learning quickly in a fast-paced startup environment.




WHAT IT TAKES TO EXCEL

 - Ownership mindset: proactive, resourceful, and end-to-end problem solving.

 - Strong product thinking and curiosity about banking infrastructure.

 - Commitment to building reliable, high-quality systems that scale.




WHY US?

 - Work alongside a founding team from Monzo and BigPay, bringing top-tier fintech expertise to the company.

 - Build scalable, cutting-edge programmable banking infrastructure that bridges traditional finance and digital assets.

 - Make a real impact: help shape architecture, systems, and engineering culture at an early-stage, fast-growing fintech.

 - Competitive salary and meaningful equity with room for growth.

 - Be part of a well-funded startup backed by top global investors, with a clear path to scale.

source_jobs.title: Accountant
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PAVE BANK:

Welcome to Pave Bank, where we’re not just reimagining banking—we’re transforming it! Inspired by global fintech pioneers and powered by data-driven ingenuity, we are building smarter, faster, and more accessible banking solutions that prioritize innovation and customer experience. Based in Tbilisi, Georgia, we combine the agility of a startup with the reliability of a well-regulated institution, empowering individuals and businesses to shape their financial futures.

Our culture thrives on collaboration, innovation, and a passion for cutting-edge technology. Join us on our mission to redefine what banking can be—and make a real impact in a dynamic, fast-paced environment. Together, we’ll pave the way to a brighter financial world.




YOUR MISSION HERE AT PAVE BANK:

As an Accountant at Pave Bank, you’re more than a number-cruncher; you’ll be at the heart of our financial ecosystem, ensuring accuracy, compliance and strategic insight in everything we do. With a high degree of agility and a proactive mindset, this role offers the great opportunity to navigate the complexities of fintech and to shape the financial strategies of a growing fintech startup in one of the world’s most regulated and innovative industries.




WHAT YOU’LL DO:

 - Be the Financial Pillar: Create accurate and timely financial reports (monthly, quarterly, annually, or as and when required) and ensure flawless reconciliation. Your work will shape the backbone of our financial health.

 - Strategic Thinker: Analyze complex financial data, identify trends, and turn numbers into actionable strategies that drive business decisions.

 - Regulatory Champion: Prepare statutory filings/ad hoc reports and submit to National Bank of Georgia and other regulators as required, ensuring we meet every requirement with precision and timeliness.

 - Audit Ace: Lead annual and interim audits, championing transparency and compliance.

 - Collaborative Innovator: Be a Business Partner across teams such as Tech, Product, Treasury and beyond to build a robust financial framework that supports our growth.

 - Policy Guardian: Keep us aligned with company policies and government regulations, ensuring every transaction is as clean as it is compliant.




TO BE SUCCESSFUL:

 - A degree in Finance, Accounting or related professional qualification, showcasing your expertise (CA, ACCA, etc).

 - Strong knowledge in International Financial Reporting Standards especially IFRS 9 and financial instruments

 - At least 3-4 years of experience in Big 4 Accounting Firms - bonus points for fintech or financial services exposure!

 - Advanced Excel skills (including macros and Power Query) and the ability to handle large data sets like a pro.

 - The ability to thrive in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment without breaking a sweat.

 - A sharp eye for detail, paired with an innovative and solution-driven mindset.

 - A love for teamwork but the confidence to work independently with tight deadlines and minimum supervision when needed.




WHY US?

 - Cutting-Edge Innovation: Work at the forefront of fintech, where every day brings fresh challenges and opportunities.

 - Dynamic Team: Collaborate with passionate, driven professionals who love what they do.

 - Startup Energy, Solid Foundation: Enjoy the agility of a startup with the stability of a regulated financial institution.

 - Your Voice Matters: At Pave Bank, your ideas and contributions directly impact our growth and success.

source_jobs.title: Director of Institutional Sales
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PAVE BANK

Welcome to Pave Bank, where we’re not just redefining banking—we’re shaping the future of global institutional finance. Inspired by leading fintech innovators and powered by a technology-first approach, we deliver smarter, faster, and more accessible banking, treasury, FX, and digital asset solutions to sophisticated institutions worldwide.

With hubs in Singapore and Tbilisi, we combine the agility of a startup with the rigor and reliability of a regulated financial institution. Our mission is to simplify complex cross-border operations, enabling exchanges, funds, fintechs, and corporates to unlock growth and seize new opportunities.

Our culture thrives on collaboration, commercial ingenuity, and a passion for solving the toughest institutional challenges. Joining Pave Bank means shaping global banking solutions, closing transformative deals, and making a tangible impact in a fast-moving, high-stakes environment. Together, we’re paving the way for the next generation of institutional finance.




ABOUT THE ROLE

The ideal candidate will be a high-calibre institutional sales professional responsible for driving Pave Bank’s institutional growth and contributing to commercial strategy. You will lead the full institutional sales lifecycle - originating and closing high-value opportunities, structuring complex multi-currency and digital asset solutions, and deepening relationships with exchanges, OTC desks, payment institutions, funds, fintechs, and corporates. Working closely with senior leadership, you will ensure institutional insights inform commercial priorities and collaborate with product, treasury, and engineering teams to deliver scalable solutions for sophisticated, high-volume clients. The role will also help lay the foundation for future team development as Pave Bank scales.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Drive institutional adoption of Pave Bank’s multi-currency banking, FX, settlement, digital asset, and treasury capabilities.

 - Lead and close complex, high-value institutional opportunities across exchanges, OTC desks, payment institutions, funds, and fintechs.

 - Structure multi-entity and cross-border solutions, including settlement, liquidity, and custody workflows.

 - Develop commercial proposals, pricing structures, and revenue models that support Pave Bank’s growth strategy.

 - Translate sophisticated institutional requirements into scalable banking and digital asset solutions in collaboration with product, treasury, and engineering teams.

 - Shape client strategy through market mapping, institutional insights, and expansion opportunities across key verticals.

 - Contribute to commercial playbooks, sales methodologies, and scalable processes as the bank grows.

 - Support development of junior talent and future team scale as the organisation expands.




EXPERIENCE

 - 10 to 15 years of enterprise sales or institutional coverage in banking, payments, fintech, or digital assets.

 - Proven ability to originate and close complex, high-value institutional accounts.

 - Strong understanding of cross-border settlement, treasury flows, liquidity structures, custody frameworks, and digital asset operations.

 - Strong financial, commercial, and pricing skills.

 - Comfortable operating in a fast-moving, scaling banking environment.

source_jobs.title: Senior Site Reliability Engineer
source_jobs.description:
The Role

Pave Bank is building the future of programmable banking — combining traditional banking with digital assets under a single, regulated platform. We’re looking for a Site Reliability Engineer (SRE) to ensure our core systems are highly available, scalable, and performant as we grow.

As an SRE at Pave Bank, you’ll work closely with Engineering, Product, Security and Operations teams to build robust infrastructure, automate operations, and maintain reliability across all services. Your work will directly impact the safety, performance, and scalability of our banking platform, helping our customers trust Pave Bank with their finances.



What You’ll Be Doing

 - Monitor, maintain, and improve the reliability, availability, and performance of production systems and services.

 - Build and maintain infrastructure as code (IaC), deployment pipelines, and automation to support continuous delivery, scalability, and disaster recovery.

 - Respond to incidents, perform root-cause analysis, and drive postmortems to ensure lessons learned are applied.

 - Implement and enforce operational best practices: observability, logging, metrics, alerting, capacity planning, failover strategies, and backups.

 - Collaborate with Engineering, Product, Compliance, and Operations teams to ensure infrastructure meets reliability, compliance, and security standards.

 - Support service scaling, database operations, cloud infrastructure (GCP preferred), networking, and microservices orchestration.

 - Document operational runbooks, on-call procedures, and system architecture to support maintenance, knowledge sharing, and compliance.



What You’ll Bring

Technical Skills and Experience

 - Strong programming or scripting skills (Go, Python, Bash, or similar) for automation, tooling, and operational tasks.

 - Hands-on experience with cloud infrastructure, ideally Google Cloud Platform (GCP).

 - Familiarity with containerization and orchestration (Docker, Kubernetes, or equivalent).

 - Experience with infrastructure-as-code tools (Terraform, Cloud Deployment Manager, or similar).

 - Experience with either FluxCD or ArgoCD for GitOps-based delivery.

 - Solid understanding of distributed systems, microservices architecture, and reliability patterns.

 - Experience setting up monitoring, logging, alerting, and observability (e.g., Prometheus, Grafana, ELK, distributed tracing).

 - Strong troubleshooting skills and ability to respond to incidents under pressure.

 - Knowledge of backup and disaster recovery strategies, database management, and secure operations.



Other Skills

 - Ownership mindset: proactive, responsible, and committed to system reliability.

 - Strong communication skills — able to coordinate across technical and non-technical stakeholders.

 - Comfortable working in a fast-paced, early-stage startup environment.

 - High integrity, attention to detail, and passion for fintech and programmable banking systems.



Nice to Have

 - Prior experience in fintech, banking, or other highly regulated industries.

 - Familiarity with compliance, security, and data protection best practices.

 - Experience with high-availability, high-throughput systems, or financial infrastructure.

 - Exposure to blockchain or crypto systems integrated with banking.

 - Experience optimizing cloud infrastructure for cost and performance under rapid growth.



Why Pave Bank?

 - Work alongside a founding team from Monzo and BigPay, bringing top-tier fintech expertise.

 - Tackle real-world reliability challenges in a regulated, fast-growing fintech environment.

 - Learn from and collaborate with experienced engineers while developing your SRE career.

 - Competitive salary and meaningful equity with room for growth.

 - Be part of a well-funded startup shaping the future of programmable banking.

source_jobs.title: Senior QA Automation Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PAVE BANK

Pave Bank is the world’s first programmable business bank. Built by the founders behind Monzo and BigPay, we’re redefining global business banking by combining programmable financial infrastructure with world-class client experiences.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for an experienced Software QA Automation Engineer to join our growing engineering team in Tbilisi. You’ll play a key role in ensuring the quality and reliability of our digital banking platforms through automated testing frameworks. This is an exciting opportunity to work in a fast-paced environment, contribute to a modern automation infrastructure, and collaborate closely with developers, and product teams.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design, develop, and maintain automated test suites using Playwright and TypeScript.

 - Collaborate with development teams to identify, document, and resolve issues throughout the software lifecycle.

 - Implement and optimize test automation as part of the CI/CD pipeline for faster and more reliable releases.

 - Perform regression, integration, and end-to-end testing across web and API layers.

 - Monitor and maintain test environments and results to ensure continuous test coverage.

 - Contribute to improving QA standards, documentation, and best practices within the team.

 - Participate in sprint planning, daily standups, and retrospectives as part of an agile workflow.




REQUIREMENTS

 - Proven professional experience in both manual and automated QA—you know how to break things and prevent bugs before they reach users.

 - Hands-on experience building automation frameworks from scratch, ideally using Playwright and TypeScript (or similar tools).

 - Strong experience integrating automated tests into CI/CD pipelines (e.g., GitHub Actions, GitLab CI).

 - Solid understanding of software development, testing principles, and Agile/DevOps workflows.

 - Experience testing web applications, APIs, and mobile apps (React Native experience a plus).

 - Experience in fast-paced startup environments and the ability to define QA strategy, processes, and standards from the ground up.

 - Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills in English.

 - Proactive, adaptable, and comfortable taking ownership in a rapidly scaling environment.




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience with performance or security testing tools.

 - Background in fintech or digital banking helpful but not essential.




WHY US?

 - We recently raised $39 million in our Series A (led by Accel), positioning the company for major growth and giving you a rare chance to join while things are still moving fast

 - Work alongside a founding team from Monzo and BigPay, bringing top-tier fintech expertise to the company.

 - Take ownership of QA automation from the ground up: design the strategy, choose the tools, and build frameworks that will shape how we ship products.

 - Influence the way we build, test, and release software, while collaborating with experienced engineers and growing your career in QA and automation.

source_jobs.title: Commercial Executive
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PAVE BANK

Pave Bank is a fully licensed commercial bank, regulated by the National Bank of Georgia, which is building the world’s first programmable bank. We are reimagining how global businesses access and move capital by bridging the gap between traditional finance and digital assets. Joining Pave Bank means becoming part of a fast-growing institution redefining this convergence. You will have the opportunity to work alongside second-time founders and industry leaders, gain deep exposure to institutional banking and digital assets, and grow within a dynamic and ambitious team.




THE ROLE

We are seeking a Commercial Executive to join our team in Tbilisi or Dubai (Visa sponsorship available). Reporting to the VP Commercial, you will focus on driving deal origination and business development with institutional clients, while ensuring a smooth and professional experience throughout the onboarding journey.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Originate and qualify new commercial opportunities across fintech, digital assets, payments, and other B2B financial services sectors.

 - Manage the full origination lifecycle: from prospecting and outreach through to structuring opportunities with internal stakeholders, while also guiding clients through onboarding.

 - Build and maintain strong relationships with senior decision-makers at target clients.

 - Collaborate closely with internal teams (product, compliance, operations and onboarding) to deliver solutions tailored to client needs.

 - Monitor market trends in digital assets and B2B banking to identify emerging opportunities.

 - Support the VP Commercial in refining go-to-market strategies and building a scalable origination engine.
   


REQUIREMENTS

 - 3–6 years of experience in commercial, sales, or business development roles within B2B banking, fintech, or financial services.

 - Strong understanding of commercial processes and deal origination within institutional contexts.

 - Familiarity with cryptocurrency and digital assets, and how they intersect with traditional finance, is essential; prior commercial experience in these industries is strongly preferred.

 - Demonstrated ability to engage senior stakeholders and manage complex sales cycles.

 - Excellent communication and presentation skills, with fluency in English (additional languages a plus).

 - Entrepreneurial mindset and ability to thrive in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.

source_jobs.title: Commercial Operations Analyst
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PAVE BANK

Pave Bank is a fully licensed commercial bank, regulated by the National Bank of Georgia. We are reimagining how global businesses access and move capital by bridging the gap between traditional finance and digital assets. Joining Pave Bank means becoming part of a fast-growing institution redefining this convergence. You will have the opportunity to work alongside second-time founders and industry leaders, gain deep exposure to institutional banking and digital assets, and grow within a dynamic and ambitious team.




THE ROLE

We are seeking a Commercial Operations Analyst to join our team in Tbilisi (Visa sponsorship available). This role will support the commercial team in building scalable processes, managing client pipelines, and ensuring smooth operations as we expand globally.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Support commercial reporting and prepare insights for the leadership team to guide decision-making.

 - Assist in the client onboarding process, coordinating across internal teams to ensure a smooth experience.

 - Track and maintain the commercial pipeline, ensuring accurate records of client opportunities.

 - Manage and optimize CRM workflows to improve visibility and efficiency.

 - Contribute to process improvement initiatives, identifying ways to streamline commercial operations.

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams to support business development activities.




REQUIREMENTS

 - Prior experience in commercial, operations, or analytical roles is strongly preferred.

 - Previous experience working in startups or fast-paced environments is strongly preferred.

 - Strong analytical and organizational skills, with attention to detail.

 - Proficiency in Excel/Google Sheets, familiarity with CRM tools and utilising AI tools and automation software (Make, n8n) to create systems.

 - Excellent communication skills in English (additional languages a plus).

 - Knowledge of digital assets and cryptocurrency is a plus, but not required.

 - Proactive mindset and eagerness to learn in a dynamic environment.

source_jobs.title: Corporate Finance & Strategy Analyst
source_jobs.description:
About Pave Bank



Pave Bank is a fully licensed commercial bank, regulated by the National Bank of Georgia. We are reimagining how global businesses access and move capital by bridging the gap between traditional finance and digital assets. Joining Pave Bank means becoming part of a fast-growing institution redefining this convergence. You will have the opportunity to work alongside second-time founders and industry leaders, gain deep exposure to institutional banking and digital assets, and grow within a dynamic and ambitious team.


The Role

We are seeking a highly motivated Corporate Finance & Strategy Analyst/Associate to join our growing team. This role offers a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of finance, strategy, and business growth, partnering closely with senior leadership on high-impact initiatives across the organisation.

The successful candidate will play a key role in supporting strategic finance projects, fundraising activities, financial planning, and corporate development initiatives. This position will involve regular travel to Georgia & Dubai providing exposure to international markets and cross-functional stakeholders.


Key Responsibilities

 - Group Strategy: Contribute to the development of medium-to long-term strategic initiatives through structured analysis, market research, and financial modelling to support key business decisions.

 - Financial Automation: Identify opportunities to improve and automate financial workflows, reporting, and data tracking using analytical tools.

 - FP&A: Perform in-depth analysis of key business drivers, including revenue, costs, and unit economics; support budgeting, forecasting, variance analysis, and management reporting.

 - Corporate Development: Support evaluation of strategic opportunities, including acquisitions, partnerships, and M&A, through market research, financial analysis, and execution support on an ad hoc basis.

 - Investor Relations: Support end-to-end fundraising efforts, including building financial models, preparing investor materials (pitch decks, data rooms, updates), and responding to ad hoc investor requests and diligence queries.



Requirements

 - Highly analytical, with experience in financial modelling and preparing strategic or investor-facing materials (experience in venture capital, investment banking, or consulting is a plus).

 - Previous experience working in startups or fast-paced environments is strongly preferred.

 - Basic understanding of accounting principles and financial statements (P&L, balance sheet, cash flow).

 - Proficiency in Excel or Google Sheets; familiarity with CRM tools and leveraging AI/automation tools is a plus.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English (additional languages are a plus).

 - Knowledge of digital assets and cryptocurrency is a plus, but not required.

source_jobs.title: Onboarding Specialist (with Compliance Knowledge)
source_jobs.description:
ROLE OVERVIEW

We are looking for an Onboarding Specialist with strong compliance knowledge to manage corporate client onboarding end-to-end. This role requires a balance between regulatory rigor and business understanding, ensuring smooth onboarding while meeting all KYC/AML requirements.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Manage end-to-end onboarding of corporate clients

 - Perform KYC, CDD, and UBO identification and verification

 - Assess Source of Wealth (SOW) and Source of Funds (SOF), with focus on SOW

 - Review company financials and supporting documentation

 - Analyze bank statements to understand business activity and relationships

 - Independently gather and verify information from registries and open sources

 - Identify ownership structures and controlling persons

 - Handle onboarding for different legal entities (LLC, JSC, LP, etc.)

 - Ensure compliance with internal policies and regulatory requirements

 - Collaborate with commercial teams to support client onboarding

 - Escalate risks, inconsistencies, or suspicious cases




REQUIREMENTS

 - 4+ years of experience in onboarding, KYC, or compliance

 - Strong understanding of UBO, ownership structures, and control

 - Experience working with corporate clients

 - Familiarity with Georgian Public Registry and other verification tools

 - Ability to analyze financial documents and bank statements

 - Strong investigative and problem-solving skills

 - Ability to work independently and take ownership

 - Fluent English (mandatory)

 - Good communication skills (client-facing + internal)




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience in banking, fintech, or payments

 - Knowledge of AML regulations and international standards

 - Experience with complex or multi-layered company structures

source_jobs.title: Product Manager, Strategic Initiatives
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PAVE BANK

Pave Bank is building modern financial infrastructure across payments, banking, trading, and digital asset services. We operate at the intersection of regulated finance, technology, and global capital movement, with a focus on creating products that are operationally robust, commercially scalable, and technically sophisticated.

We are looking for a Product Manager who is comfortable operating in ambiguity, translating complex business problems into executable product and engineering outcomes, and helping shape new business verticals from the ground up.

This is not a traditional “roadmap maintenance” product role. The ideal candidate is commercially curious, technically literate, and excited to work across multiple domains within financial services.

Over time, we expect this role to evolve into ownership of a business unit or strategic product vertical.

 




WHAT YOU’LL DO

Drive Cross-Functional Product Execution

 - Work closely with leadership, operations, compliance, partnerships, and engineering teams to shape and launch new capabilities.

 - Translate business needs, operational workflows, and strategic goals into clear product requirements and execution plans.

 - Partner with engineers on product design, technical tradeoffs, implementation sequencing, and delivery.

 - Help define APIs, workflows, operational tooling, and user experiences across financial products.

Operate Across Multiple Financial Domains

You may work across:

 - Cross-border payments

 - Banking infrastructure

 - Trading and liquidity products

 - Investment-related initiatives

 - Treasury and settlement workflows

 - Client-facing financial tooling

The role requires comfort switching contexts quickly and developing working fluency in multiple parts of the financial stack.

Strategic and Commercial Thinking

 - Identify opportunities for new product lines, operational efficiencies, or market expansion.

 - Contribute to business cases, market sizing, pricing considerations, and go-to-market thinking.

 - Work with stakeholders to evaluate commercial viability and operational feasibility of new initiatives.

 - Think beyond feature delivery and understand how products contribute to revenue, risk, and long-term company positioning.

Communication & Alignment

 - Act as the bridge between business and technical teams.

 - Bring clarity to ambiguous problems and align stakeholders around priorities and execution.

 - Communicate effectively with both highly technical engineers and commercially-focused stakeholders.

 


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Strong Product & Business Judgment

 - Ability to break down ambiguous business problems into structured execution plans.

 - Strong intuition for prioritization, tradeoffs, and operational practicality.

 - Commercial mindset with interest in how financial products create value.

Technical Fluency

You do not need to be an engineer, but you should be comfortable:

 - Working closely with backend and infrastructure teams

 - Understanding APIs, workflows, and system dependencies

 - Discussing implementation tradeoffs with engineers

 - Writing clear and structured product requirements

Adaptability

 - Comfortable operating in fast-moving environments with evolving priorities.

 - Able to move between strategic discussions and execution details.

 - Curious and proactive in unfamiliar domains.

Communication Skills

 - Clear written and verbal communication.

 - Ability to synthesize complex topics into actionable decisions.

 - Comfortable engaging with senior stakeholders and cross-functional teams.

 

Nice to Have

 - Experience in fintech, banking, payments, trading, or financial services

 - Exposure to regulated environments

 - Experience working with engineering teams on backend-heavy products

 - Background in consulting, startups, strategy, operations, or technical product management

 - Experience building 0→1 products or new business initiatives

 


WHY THIS ROLE IS DIFFEREN

source_jobs.title: Client Success Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PAVE BANK:

Pave Bank is the world’s first programmable business bank. Built by the founders behind Monzo and BigPay, we are redefining global business banking by integrating programmable financial infrastructure with world-class client experiences.




ABOUT CLIENT SUCCESS:

The Client Success team ensures our clients successfully adopt, operate, and scale using Pave’s banking platform.

We sit at the intersection of clients, operations, engineering, and compliance, ensuring that our services are delivered reliably while continuously improving operational efficiency and client experience.

Our goal is to ensure clients derive maximum value from Pave’s platform while maintaining the highest operational and regulatory standards.




ROLE OVERVIEW

As a Client Success Lead, you will be responsible for managing the operational lifecycle of client relationships—from onboarding through ongoing support and operational optimization.

You will ensure clients successfully implement and operate Pave’s banking products while driving operational excellence across client workflows. The role requires strong cross-functional collaboration with Product, Engineering, Compliance, and Risk teams to ensure client needs are addressed while maintaining regulatory integrity.

This role combines client relationship ownership, operational problem-solving, and data-driven decision-making.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES OF CLIENT SUCCESS LEAD:

 - Client Management: Proactively identify opportunities to improve client adoption and operational efficiency.

 - Account Management: Build strong, trusted relationships with client stakeholders to ensure a high-quality service experience.

 - Revenue & Retention: Support initiatives that drive Net Revenue Retention (NRR) through strong client engagement and product adoption.

 - Identify expansion opportunities, including cross-sell and upsell of additional Pave services.

 - Operational Excellence: Identify operational bottlenecks and implement scalable solutions to improve service delivery. Contribute to building internal operational documentation and knowledge bases.

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration: Act as the "voice of the customer" internally, working directly with Engineering, Operations, and Compliance teams to prioritize client-critical initiatives and influence the product roadmap.

 - Risk & Compliance Integration: Work closely with Risk and Compliance teams to ensure all client activities and expansions align with regulatory standards in a complex banking environment.

 - Data Analysis: Monitor client usage patterns and health scores to make proactive operational and resourcing decisions.

 - Client Success Excellence: Design, implement, and optimize the Client Success workflow and strategies to drive efficiency and data-driven decisions. Contribute to internal knowledge-sharing and help build the playbooks for onboarding, adoption, and revenue generation.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Experience: 6+ years of progressive experience in Client Success, Account Management, Client Success, managing enterprise or fintech clients in complex operational environments.

 - Sector Background: Proven track record in a complex Banking/ Digital Banking, FinTech, SaaS, or Software.

 - Operational & Compliance Knowledge: Hands-on experience working within Risk and Compliance frameworks to manage client portfolios safely.

 - Data & Analytics: Highly analytical and comfortable using data—such as financial projections and health scores—to drive operational results.

 - Global Mindset: Ability to manage international portfolios and work effectively across diverse, global teams.

 - Communication: Exceptional communication and negotiation skills, with the ability to influence internal stakeholders and external client leaders.

 - Adaptability: Comfort working in a fast-paced, high-growth environment where agility is key.

 - Languages: Fluent in English; Mandarin or any additional languages are considered a bonus

source_jobs.title: Business Analyst
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

Pave Bank is hiring a Business Analyst to support the Product team in analysing business needs, documenting requirements, improving processes, and supporting the delivery of banking products, internal tools, and operational workflows.

This is a great opportunity for someone who wants to work at the intersection of product, banking operations, compliance, payments, and technology in a regulated banking environment.

In this role, you will work closely with Product, Engineering, QA, Operations, Compliance, and other internal teams to understand business needs, analyse workflows, define requirements, and support product delivery from discovery through implementation and testing.

We are looking for someone who is structured, detail-oriented, analytical, and comfortable working independently in a fast-moving environment. The ideal candidate should already have experience in a Business Analyst, Product Analyst, QA, Operations Analyst, or similar role, and be able to take ownership of requirements, documentation, and process analysis.

Experience in fintech, banking, payments, compliance, operations, or digital products will be highly valued.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Gather, analyse, and document business and product requirements.

 - Translate business needs into clear user stories, workflows, acceptance criteria, and functional specifications.

 - Analyse current processes and identify gaps, risks, dependencies, and improvement opportunities.

 - Work closely with Engineering and QA to clarify requirements and validate implementation.

 - Collaborate with Operations, Compliance, Commercial, and other stakeholders on process-heavy initiatives.

 - Maintain product documentation, process flows, and update notes where needed.

 - Help ensure requirements are clearly understood across both business and technical teams.




WE’RE LOOKING FOR SOMEONE WHO HAS

 - Previous experience as a Business Analyst, Product Analyst, QA Analyst, Operations Analyst, or in a similar role.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English are required.

 - Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

 - Ability to understand complex workflows and break them down clearly.

 - Ability to write clear, structured, and practical documentation.

 - Strong attention to detail and ownership mindset.

 - Ability to work with both business and technical teams.

 - Confidence to ask the right questions, clarify requirements, and challenge unclear processes.

 - Good organisational skills and ability to manage multiple topics at the same time.

 - Interest in banking, fintech, payments, compliance, or digital products.




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience in fintech, banking, payments, AML/KYC, compliance, or regulated financial services.

 - Understanding of APIs, payment flows, digital banking products, or internal operational systems.

 - Understanding of software development lifecycle.




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Opportunity to work in a fast-growing digital bank.

 - Hands-on experience with banking products, payments, compliance, digital assets, internal operations, and product development.

 - Close collaboration with experienced Product, Engineering, Operations, and Compliance teams.

 - Opportunity to take ownership of meaningful product and process improvements.

 - Dynamic and challenging environment with room to grow.

source_jobs.title: Corporate Finance & Strategy Analyst
source_jobs.description:
About Pave Bank



Pave Bank is a fully licensed commercial bank, regulated by the National Bank of Georgia. We are reimagining how global businesses access and move capital by bridging the gap between traditional finance and digital assets. Joining Pave Bank means becoming part of a fast-growing institution redefining this convergence. You will have the opportunity to work alongside second-time founders and industry leaders, gain deep exposure to institutional banking and digital assets, and grow within a dynamic and ambitious team.


The Role

We are seeking a highly motivated Corporate Finance & Strategy Analyst/Associate to join our growing team. This role offers a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of finance, strategy, and business growth, partnering closely with senior leadership on high-impact initiatives across the organisation.

The successful candidate will play a key role in supporting strategic finance projects, fundraising activities, financial planning, and corporate development initiatives. This position will involve regular travel to Georgia & Dubai providing exposure to international markets and cross-functional stakeholders.


Key Responsibilities

 - Group Strategy: Contribute to the development of medium-to long-term strategic initiatives through structured analysis, market research, and financial modelling to support key business decisions.

 - Financial Automation: Identify opportunities to improve and automate financial workflows, reporting, and data tracking using analytical tools.

 - FP&A: Perform in-depth analysis of key business drivers, including revenue, costs, and unit economics; support budgeting, forecasting, variance analysis, and management reporting.

 - Corporate Development: Support evaluation of strategic opportunities, including acquisitions, partnerships, and M&A, through market research, financial analysis, and execution support on an ad hoc basis.

 - Investor Relations: Support end-to-end fundraising efforts, including building financial models, preparing investor materials (pitch decks, data rooms, updates), and responding to ad hoc investor requests and diligence queries.



Requirements

 - Highly analytical, with experience in financial modelling and preparing strategic or investor-facing materials (experience in venture capital, investment banking, or consulting is a plus).

 - Previous experience working in startups or fast-paced environments is strongly preferred.

 - Basic understanding of accounting principles and financial statements (P&L, balance sheet, cash flow).

 - Proficiency in Excel or Google Sheets; familiarity with CRM tools and leveraging AI/automation tools is a plus.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English (additional languages are a plus).

 - Knowledge of digital assets and cryptocurrency is a plus, but not required.

source_jobs.title: Client Success Associate
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PAVE BANK:

Pave Bank is the world’s first programmable business bank. Built by the founders behind Monzo and BigPay, we are redefining global business banking by integrating programmable financial infrastructure with world-class client experiences.




ABOUT CLIENT SUCCESS:

At Client Success, our mission is to ensure our clients extract the maximum value from our banking products and services— driving product adoption, retention, expansion, and overall Pave experience. You will be a critical voice for the client internally and a trusted advisor externally, contributing directly to the company’s growth and product evolution.

As a Client Success Associate, you will be on the front lines of our operational engine, ensuring our global clients extract maximum value from our banking products. This is a high-impact role focused on the day-to-day execution of client retention, product adoption, and operational excellence. You will act as a bridge between our clients and our internal teams, ensuring that our programmable banking solutions are implemented seamlessly and securely.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES OF CLIENT SUCCESS ASSOCIATE:

 - Operational Management: Design, implement, and optimize Client Success workflows to drive efficiency and data-driven decisions.

 - Revenue & Retention: Execute strategies to maximize Net Revenue Retention (NRR) and identify expansion opportunities (upsell/cross-sell) of the client portfolio.

 - Onboarding & Playbooks: Contribute to internal knowledge-sharing and build operational playbooks for client onboarding and long-term adoption.

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration: Act as the "voice of the customer" internally, working directly with Tech, Operations, and Compliance teams to prioritize client-critical initiatives and influence the product roadmap.

 - Risk & Compliance Integration: Work closely with Risk and Compliance teams to ensure all client activities and expansions align with regulatory standards in a complex banking environment.

 - Data Analysis: Monitor client usage patterns and health scores to make proactive operational and resourcing decisions.

 - Client Success Excellence: Design, implement, and optimize the Client Success workflow and strategies to drive efficiency and data-driven decisions. Contribute to internal knowledge-sharing and help build the playbooks for onboarding, adoption, and revenue generation.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Experience: 3+ years of progressive experience in Customer Relations, Client Success, Account Management, or a strategic client-facing role.

 - Sector Background: Proven track record in a complex Banking/ Digital Banking, FinTech, SaaS, or Software.

 - Operational & Compliance Knowledge: Hands-on experience working within Risk and Compliance frameworks to manage client portfolios safely.

 - Data & Analytics: Highly analytical and comfortable using data—such as financial projections and health scores—to drive operational results.

 - Global Mindset: Ability to manage international portfolios and work effectively across diverse, global teams.

 - Communication: Exceptional communication and negotiation skills, with the ability to influence internal stakeholders and external client leaders.

 - Adaptability: Comfort working in a fast-paced, high-growth environment where agility is key.

 - Languages: Fluent in English; any additional languages are considered a bonus.




WHAT WE OFFER:

 - The opportunity to shape the future of programmable banking and define new standards for global business finance.

 - A mission-driven, diverse, and international team with deep domain expertise and a passion for innovation.

 - Competitive compensation and benefits package.

 - Equity in the company—so you're aligned with our growth and success.

source_jobs.title: Senior Site Reliability Engineer
source_jobs.description:
The Role

Pave Bank is building the future of programmable banking — combining traditional banking with digital assets under a single, regulated platform. We’re looking for a Site Reliability Engineer (SRE) to ensure our core systems are highly available, scalable, and performant as we grow.

As an SRE at Pave Bank, you’ll work closely with Engineering, Product, Security and Operations teams to build robust infrastructure, automate operations, and maintain reliability across all services. Your work will directly impact the safety, performance, and scalability of our banking platform, helping our customers trust Pave Bank with their finances.



What You’ll Be Doing

 - Monitor, maintain, and improve the reliability, availability, and performance of production systems and services.

 - Build and maintain infrastructure as code (IaC), deployment pipelines, and automation to support continuous delivery, scalability, and disaster recovery.

 - Respond to incidents, perform root-cause analysis, and drive postmortems to ensure lessons learned are applied.

 - Implement and enforce operational best practices: observability, logging, metrics, alerting, capacity planning, failover strategies, and backups.

 - Collaborate with Engineering, Product, Compliance, and Operations teams to ensure infrastructure meets reliability, compliance, and security standards.

 - Support service scaling, database operations, cloud infrastructure (GCP preferred), networking, and microservices orchestration.

 - Document operational runbooks, on-call procedures, and system architecture to support maintenance, knowledge sharing, and compliance.



What You’ll Bring

Technical Skills and Experience

 - Strong programming or scripting skills (Go, Python, Bash, or similar) for automation, tooling, and operational tasks.

 - Hands-on experience with cloud infrastructure, ideally Google Cloud Platform (GCP).

 - Familiarity with containerization and orchestration (Docker, Kubernetes, or equivalent).

 - Experience with infrastructure-as-code tools (Terraform, Cloud Deployment Manager, or similar).

 - Experience with either FluxCD or ArgoCD for GitOps-based delivery.

 - Solid understanding of distributed systems, microservices architecture, and reliability patterns.

 - Experience setting up monitoring, logging, alerting, and observability (e.g., Prometheus, Grafana, ELK, distributed tracing).

 - Strong troubleshooting skills and ability to respond to incidents under pressure.

 - Knowledge of backup and disaster recovery strategies, database management, and secure operations.



Other Skills

 - Ownership mindset: proactive, responsible, and committed to system reliability.

 - Strong communication skills — able to coordinate across technical and non-technical stakeholders.

 - Comfortable working in a fast-paced, early-stage startup environment.

 - High integrity, attention to detail, and passion for fintech and programmable banking systems.



Nice to Have

 - Prior experience in fintech, banking, or other highly regulated industries.

 - Familiarity with compliance, security, and data protection best practices.

 - Experience with high-availability, high-throughput systems, or financial infrastructure.

 - Exposure to blockchain or crypto systems integrated with banking.

 - Experience optimizing cloud infrastructure for cost and performance under rapid growth.



Why Pave Bank?

 - Work alongside a founding team from Monzo and BigPay, bringing top-tier fintech expertise.

 - Tackle real-world reliability challenges in a regulated, fast-growing fintech environment.

 - Learn from and collaborate with experienced engineers while developing your SRE career.

 - Competitive salary and meaningful equity with room for growth.

 - Be part of a well-funded startup shaping the future of programmable banking.

source_jobs.title: Client Success Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PAVE BANK:

Pave Bank is the world’s first programmable business bank. Built by the founders behind Monzo and BigPay, we are redefining global business banking by integrating programmable financial infrastructure with world-class client experiences.




ABOUT CLIENT SUCCESS:

The Client Success team ensures our clients successfully adopt, operate, and scale using Pave’s banking platform.

We sit at the intersection of clients, operations, engineering, and compliance, ensuring that our services are delivered reliably while continuously improving operational efficiency and client experience.

Our goal is to ensure clients derive maximum value from Pave’s platform while maintaining the highest operational and regulatory standards.




ROLE OVERVIEW

As a Client Success Lead, you will be responsible for managing the operational lifecycle of client relationships—from onboarding through ongoing support and operational optimization.

You will ensure clients successfully implement and operate Pave’s banking products while driving operational excellence across client workflows. The role requires strong cross-functional collaboration with Product, Engineering, Compliance, and Risk teams to ensure client needs are addressed while maintaining regulatory integrity.

This role combines client relationship ownership, operational problem-solving, and data-driven decision-making.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES OF CLIENT SUCCESS LEAD:

 - Client Management: Proactively identify opportunities to improve client adoption and operational efficiency.

 - Account Management: Build strong, trusted relationships with client stakeholders to ensure a high-quality service experience.

 - Revenue & Retention: Support initiatives that drive Net Revenue Retention (NRR) through strong client engagement and product adoption.

 - Identify expansion opportunities, including cross-sell and upsell of additional Pave services.

 - Operational Excellence: Identify operational bottlenecks and implement scalable solutions to improve service delivery. Contribute to building internal operational documentation and knowledge bases.

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration: Act as the "voice of the customer" internally, working directly with Engineering, Operations, and Compliance teams to prioritize client-critical initiatives and influence the product roadmap.

 - Risk & Compliance Integration: Work closely with Risk and Compliance teams to ensure all client activities and expansions align with regulatory standards in a complex banking environment.

 - Data Analysis: Monitor client usage patterns and health scores to make proactive operational and resourcing decisions.

 - Client Success Excellence: Design, implement, and optimize the Client Success workflow and strategies to drive efficiency and data-driven decisions. Contribute to internal knowledge-sharing and help build the playbooks for onboarding, adoption, and revenue generation.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Experience: 6+ years of progressive experience in Client Success, Account Management, Client Success, managing enterprise or fintech clients in complex operational environments.

 - Sector Background: Proven track record in a complex Banking/ Digital Banking, FinTech, SaaS, or Software.

 - Operational & Compliance Knowledge: Hands-on experience working within Risk and Compliance frameworks to manage client portfolios safely.

 - Data & Analytics: Highly analytical and comfortable using data—such as financial projections and health scores—to drive operational results.

 - Global Mindset: Ability to manage international portfolios and work effectively across diverse, global teams.

 - Communication: Exceptional communication and negotiation skills, with the ability to influence internal stakeholders and external client leaders.

 - Adaptability: Comfort working in a fast-paced, high-growth environment where agility is key.

 - Languages: Fluent in English; Mandarin or any additional languages are considered a bonus

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PAVE BANK

Pave Bank is the world’s first programmable business bank. Built by the founders behind Monzo and BigPay, we’re redefining global business banking by combining programmable financial infrastructure with world-class client experiences.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Pave Bank is building the next generation of programmable banking infrastructure. We are looking for a Senior Software Engineer to join our Engineering team and work closely with Product, Compliance, Treasury, and Operations.

In this role, you will play a pivotal role in shaping the backbone of Pave Bank, delivering scalable, reliable, and maintainable systems that power our core banking operations. You will design distributed systems, implement features end-to-end, mentor junior engineers, and collaborate across teams to drive high-quality software that accelerates the business.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design, build, and maintain backend services using Go or other strongly typed languages.

 - Develop scalable, event-driven distributed systems that power critical banking operations.

 - Collaborate with stakeholders (Product, Compliance, Treasury, Operations) to define requirements and deliver high-quality solutions.

 - Write detailed design documents for new features and system improvements.

 - Review pull requests and provide constructive technical feedback to peers.

 - Implement end-to-end features: architecture → coding → testing → deployment.

 - Ensure code quality through clean, maintainable, well-documented code and automated tests.

 - Contribute to engineering best practices, tooling, and system reliability improvements.




REQUIREMENTS

Technical Skills

 - Minimum 5 years of professional software engineering experience.

 - Strong proficiency in Go, or proven exceptional ability in another strongly typed language.

 - Solid understanding of distributed systems and event-driven architectures.

 - Experience in a Tier 1 tech company (e.g., Stripe, Grab, Google, Deliveroo, Monzo, Shopee, Bytedance) or significant open-source contributions.

 - Hands-on experience with Temporal, GCP, or Encore.dev http://Encore.dev is a plus.



Non-Technical Skills

 - Collaborative, empathetic, and able to communicate effectively with technical and non-technical stakeholders.

 - Strong interest in fintech, banking, or financial infrastructure.

 - Comfortable navigating ambiguity and learning quickly in a fast-paced startup environment.




WHAT IT TAKES TO EXCEL

 - Ownership mindset: proactive, resourceful, and end-to-end problem solving.

 - Strong product thinking and curiosity about banking infrastructure.

 - Commitment to building reliable, high-quality systems that scale.




WHY US?

 - Work alongside a founding team from Monzo and BigPay, bringing top-tier fintech expertise to the company.

 - Build scalable, cutting-edge programmable banking infrastructure that bridges traditional finance and digital assets.

 - Make a real impact: help shape architecture, systems, and engineering culture at an early-stage, fast-growing fintech.

 - Competitive salary and meaningful equity with room for growth.

 - Be part of a well-funded startup backed by top global investors, with a clear path to scale.

source_jobs.title: Junior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for an early-career Software Engineer to join us on a rotational track across our functional units: Banking & Payments, Trading, Investment Products, and Internal Products. You'll get broad exposure to how a programmable bank works end-to-end, then go deep where you can have the most impact.

You'll work across the stack, shipping real frontend and backend changes that hit production. Along the way you'll explore how AI tools can make you a faster, sharper engineer, though we measure you the traditional way: by your judgement, the quality of what you ship, and the impact you have.

This is a role for someone early in their career who wants to see the whole business, ship across several domains, and grow quickly into a high-ownership technical contributor.




WHAT YOU'LL DO


IN YOUR FIRST 6 MONTHS

 - Rotate across functional units (Banking & Payments, Trading, Investment Products, Internal Products), shipping full-stack changes and learning how each domain fits into the wider platform

 - Pick up well-scoped features and bugs, take them from ticket to production, and learn our codebase, tooling, and review culture

 - Work closely with senior engineers across teams to understand the systems you're touching and the trade-offs behind them

 - Use AI tools as part of your everyday workflow, and help us figure out where they genuinely improve engineering velocity and quality


AS YOU GROW

 - Own small, well-defined domains end-to-end: designing, building, shipping, and maintaining them

 - Take responsibility for delivery outcomes, not just code, in the product line where you can drive the most value

 - Participate in code reviews, technical design discussions, and RFCs, and start contributing your own

 - Improve the systems and internal tooling around you, raising the bar for everyone who works in the same code




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

 - At 6 months: you've established yourself in a product line, own one or more small domains within it, and can be trusted to take a problem and deliver an outcome.

 - Within a year: you've grown into a mid-level engineer, owning larger pieces of work, making sound technical decisions on your own, and driving measurable impact on your team's roadmap.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 0-2 years of experience in software development

 - Comfort (or genuine eagerness to get comfortable) working across the stack, including the frontend

 - Proficiency in at least one language (Go and TypeScript preferred but not essential)

 - Strong problem-solving skills and sound engineering judgement, even on small problems

 - Curiosity about how systems fit together and how the business actually makes money

 - Interest in using AI tools well

 - Growth mindset: eager to learn, iterate, take feedback, and take on more responsibility

We care more about your judgement, curiosity, and ability to ship than about how many years you've been coding. More than half our engineers come from non-technical or non-conventional backgrounds, including plenty of career switchers, and we don't require a computer science degree (or any degree). We're not indexing on your ability to architect complex systems just yet; that comes with time and mentorship. What's central to the ones who succeed at Pave is the desire to grow, deep curiosity about how things work (both the engineering and the business), communication (written and verbal), and real ownership of delivery outcomes. If your interests lean towards pure backend engineering at a senior level, we're also hiring senior backend engineers, so please reach out if that's you.




QUESTIONS THAT SHOULD EXCITE YOU

 - How do we move money reliably and safely at scale, and what happens when an edge case slips through?

 - What separates an API that platforms love to build on from one they tolerate?

 - Why does this work in staging but break in production, and how do I make sure it never happens again?

 - Where do AI tools genuinely make me faste

notes: High tech role ratio, multiple engineering positions, and a strong focus on programmable banking infrastructure suggest an engineering-heavy primary function.
